| StylusPlugIn | OnStylusDownProcessed | `protected virtual void OnStylusDownProcessed(RawStylusInput rawStylusInput)` | `protected virtual void OnStylusDownProcessed(object callbackData, bool targetVerified)` | param-type | Match WPF signature: two parameters (object callbackData, bool targetVerified) instead of a single RawStylusInput. Jalium's processed-callback redesign changes both the parameter count (2->1) and types. |
| StylusPlugIn | OnStylusMoveProcessed | `protected virtual void OnStylusMoveProcessed(RawStylusInput rawStylusInput)` | `protected virtual void OnStylusMoveProcessed(object callbackData, bool targetVerified)` | param-type | Match WPF signature: (object callbackData, bool targetVerified) instead of (RawStylusInput). Differs in both parameter count and type. |
| StylusPlugIn | OnStylusUpProcessed | `protected virtual void OnStylusUpProcessed(RawStylusInput rawStylusInput)` | `protected virtual void OnStylusUpProcessed(object callbackData, bool targetVerified)` | param-type | Match WPF signature: (object callbackData, bool targetVerified) instead of (RawStylusInput). Differs in both parameter count and type. |
| D3DImage | IsFrontBufferAvailableChanged | `public event System.EventHandler? IsFrontBufferAvailableChanged` | `public event System.Windows.DependencyPropertyChangedEventHandler IsFrontBufferAvailableChanged` | param-type | Change the event delegate type from EventHandler to DependencyPropertyChangedEventHandler to match WPF. |
| D3DImage | TryLock | `public bool TryLock(System.TimeSpan timeout)` | `public bool TryLock(System.Windows.Duration timeout)` | param-type | Accept System.Windows.Duration instead of System.TimeSpan. |
| HwndHost | HwndHost (declared base/interface list) | `public abstract class HwndHost : FrameworkElement, IDisposable` | `public abstract class HwndHost : FrameworkElement, IDisposable, IKeyboardInputSink, IWin32Window` | kind-mismatch | Implement System.Windows.Interop.IKeyboardInputSink and IWin32Window (mapped Jalium.UI.Interop) plus their explicit members (KeyboardInputSite, HasFocusWithin, OnMnemonic, RegisterKeyboardInputSink, TabInto, TranslateAccelerator, TranslateChar) to match the WPF keyboard-sink contract. |
| HwndSource | HwndSource (type declaration) | `public sealed class HwndSource : System.IDisposable` | `public partial class HwndSource : System.Windows.PresentationSource, System.IDisposable, System.Windows.Interop.IKeyboardInputSink, System.Windows.Interop.IWin32Window` | kind-mismatch | Derive HwndSource from PresentationSource and implement IKeyboardInputSink + IWin32Window; drop 'sealed' so the protected virtual *Core overrides (GetCompositionTargetCore, TabIntoCore, TranslateAcceleratorCore, etc.) are actually overridable. This base-type divergence is the root cause of ~15 missing members. |
| HwndSourceParameters | HwndSourceParameters | `public sealed class HwndSourceParameters` | `public partial struct HwndSourceParameters` | kind-mismatch | Declare as a struct (value type) to match WPF value semantics; WPF passes it by value and relies on Equals/GetHashCode/operator ==. As a class Jalium loses copy-on-assign semantics. |
| InteropBitmap | Invalidate | `public void Invalidate(Jalium.UI.Media.Int32Rect sourceRect)` | `public void Invalidate(System.Windows.Int32Rect? dirtyRect)` | param-type | Make the parameter a nullable Int32Rect? to match WPF, where null invalidates the whole surface. Jalium's non-nullable overload cannot express the whole-surface case. |
| ContainerUIElement3D | Children | `public System.Collections.Generic.IList<Jalium.UI.Media.Media3D.Visual3D> Children { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Visual3DCollection Children { get; }` | return-type | Introduce a Visual3DCollection type and return it instead of IList<Visual3D>; also apply [DesignerSerializationVisibility(Content)]. |
| ContainerUIElement3D | Visual3DChildrenCount | `public int Visual3DChildrenCount { get; }  // inherited from Jalium Visual3D base, non-virtual, NOT overridden here` | `protected override int Visual3DChildrenCount { get; }` | accessibility | Make Visual3D.Visual3DChildrenCount protected virtual and override it protected on ContainerUIElement3D so it reflects this element's own children. |
| ContainerUIElement3D | GetVisual3DChild | `public Jalium.UI.Media.Media3D.Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index)  // inherited from Jalium Visual3D base, non-virtual, NOT overridden here` | `protected override System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index)` | accessibility | Make Visual3D.GetVisual3DChild protected virtual and override it protected on ContainerUIElement3D. |
| GeneralTransform3DTo2D | GeneralTransform3DTo2D | `public sealed class GeneralTransform3DTo2D  // no base; internal ctor takes Matrix3D` | `public partial class GeneralTransform3DTo2D : System.Windows.Freezable` | kind-mismatch | Derive from Freezable (or Jalium's equivalent) and drop 'sealed' to match WPF's extensible, freezable design. |
| MaterialGroup | Children | `public System.Collections.Generic.List<Jalium.UI.Media.Media3D.Material> Children { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.MaterialCollection Children { get; set; }` | return-type | Return a MaterialCollection (the missing Freezable-backed collection) and expose a public setter to match WPF's get/set DependencyProperty-backed Children; Jalium currently exposes a read-only List<Material>. |
| Matrix3D | M11..M44, OffsetX, OffsetY, OffsetZ (16 matrix elements) | `public double M11;  (public field, x16)` | `public double M11 { get; set; }  (property, x16)` | kind-mismatch | Expose the 16 matrix elements as get/set properties instead of public fields to match WPF (enables data binding, TypeConverter, serialization). |
| Model3DGroup | Children | `public List<Model3D> Children { get; }` | `public Model3DCollection Children { get; set; }` | return-type | Introduce Model3DCollection and expose Children as a settable DP-backed property; Jalium's is a get-only List<Model3D> (no setter, wrong collection type). |
| ModelVisual3D | Children | `public IList<Visual3D> Children { get; }` | `public Visual3DCollection Children { get; }` | return-type | Introduce a Visual3DCollection type and return it from Children instead of IList<Visual3D> (also missing [DesignerSerializationVisibility(Content)]). |
| ModelVisual3D | Visual3DChildrenCount | `public int Visual3DChildrenCount (inherited from Visual3D, non-virtual)` | `protected sealed override int Visual3DChildrenCount { get; }` | accessibility | Make Visual3D.Visual3DChildrenCount protected virtual and seal-override it here; Jalium exposes it public non-virtual on the base. |
| ModelVisual3D | GetVisual3DChild | `public Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index) (inherited from Visual3D, non-virtual)` | `protected sealed override Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index)` | accessibility | Make Visual3D.GetVisual3DChild protected virtual and seal-override it here; Jalium exposes it public non-virtual on the base. |
| Point3D | operator * | `public static Point3D operator *(Point3D point, double scalar)` | `public static Point3D operator *(Point3D point, Matrix3D matrix)` | param-type | WPF's operator * multiplies a Point3D by a Matrix3D; Jalium instead defines a non-WPF Point3D*double scalar operator. Add the Matrix3D overload (the scalar overload is a Jalium-only extension). |
| Point3DCollection | Point3DCollection (base type / kind) | `public sealed class Point3DCollection : List<Point3D>` | `public sealed class Point3DCollection : Freezable, IList<Point3D>, IFormattable, ...` | kind-mismatch | Reimplement as a Freezable-backed collection (IList<Point3D>/IFormattable with a nested Point3DCollection.Enumerator) rather than subclassing List<Point3D>; add Parse, ToString(IFormatProvider), and the Freezable clone/freeze overrides. |
| Point3DCollection | GetEnumerator | `public List<Point3D>.Enumerator GetEnumerator() (inherited from List<T>)` | `public Point3DCollection.Enumerator GetEnumerator()` | return-type | WPF exposes a dedicated public nested Enumerator struct; Jalium inherits List<T>.Enumerator. Usable in foreach, but the public nested Point3DCollection.Enumerator type is absent. |
| Rect3D | Location | `public Point3D Location { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Point3D Location { get; set; }` | accessibility | Add a public set accessor that writes X/Y/Z from the assigned Point3D; WPF's Location is read-write. |
| Rect3D | Size | `public Size3D Size { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Size3D Size { get; set; }` | accessibility | Add a public set accessor that writes SizeX/SizeY/SizeZ from the assigned Size3D; WPF's Size is read-write. |
| Transform3DGroup | Children | `public System.Collections.Generic.List<Transform3D> Children { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Transform3DCollection Children { get; set; }` | return-type | Change Children to type Transform3DCollection and add a setter (get; set;). Jalium currently exposes a get-only List<Transform3D>. |
| Vector3D | Vector3D | `public struct Vector3D : System.IEquatable<Vector3D>` | `public partial struct Vector3D : System.IFormattable` | generic-constraint | WPF Vector3D implements IFormattable (not IEquatable); Jalium implements IEquatable<Vector3D> and omits IFormattable. Add IFormattable + ToString(IFormatProvider) to match the WPF contract. |
| Vector3DCollection | Vector3DCollection | `public sealed class Vector3DCollection : System.Collections.Generic.List<Vector3D>` | `public sealed partial class Vector3DCollection : System.Windows.Freezable, IList<Vector3D>, ICollection<Vector3D>, ..., System.IFormattable` | kind-mismatch | WPF derives from Freezable and implements IFormattable; Jalium derives from List<Vector3D>. List<T> satisfies the IList/ICollection/IEnumerable surface (Count, indexer, Add, Clear, Contains, CopyTo, IndexOf, Insert, Remove, RemoveAt, GetEnumerator) and all 3 ctors, but omits the Freezable clone/freeze infra, Parse, ToString overrides, IFormattable, and the public nested Enumerator struct. |
| Viewport2DVisual3D | Visual | `public object? Visual { get; set; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Visual Visual { get; set; }` | return-type | Type the Visual property as the Jalium Visual (2D) type instead of object to match WPF's System.Windows.Media.Visual. |
| Viewport3DVisual | Children | `public System.Collections.Generic.IList<Visual3D> Children { get; }` | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Visual3DCollection Children { get; }` | return-type | WPF exposes a Visual3DCollection (a type Jalium lacks); Jalium returns IList<Visual3D>. Introduce Visual3DCollection or accept the IList divergence. |
| Visual3D | Visual3DChildrenCount | `public int Visual3DChildrenCount { get; }` | `protected virtual int Visual3DChildrenCount { get; }` | accessibility | WPF exposes this as protected virtual (overridable by derived Visual3Ds); Jalium makes it public and non-virtual. Make it protected virtual to match. |
| Visual3D | GetVisual3DChild | `public System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index)` | `protected virtual System.Windows.Media.Media3D.Visual3D GetVisual3DChild(int index)` | accessibility | WPF is protected virtual; Jalium is public non-virtual. Make it protected virtual to match. |
| CustomContentState | JournalEntryName | `public abstract string JournalEntryName { get; }` | `public virtual string JournalEntryName { get; }` | kind-mismatch | WPF declares JournalEntryName as virtual with a default implementation; Jalium made it abstract, forcing every subclass to implement it. Change to virtual with a sensible default to match WPF. |
| CustomContentState | Replay | `public abstract void Replay(object content, NavigationMode mode)` | `public abstract void Replay(System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationService navigationService, System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationMode mode)` | param-type | First parameter type differs: WPF passes a NavigationService, Jalium passes object content. Change the first parameter to NavigationService for signature parity. |
| NavigationService | FragmentNavigation | `public event System.EventHandler<FragmentNavigationEventArgs>? FragmentNavigation` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.FragmentNavigationEventHandler FragmentNavigation` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate FragmentNavigationEventHandler(object sender, FragmentNavigationEventArgs e) and use it as the event type instead of EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationService | LoadCompleted | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? LoadCompleted` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.LoadCompletedEventHandler LoadCompleted` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate LoadCompletedEventHandler(object sender, NavigationEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationService | Navigated | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? Navigated` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigatedEventHandler Navigated` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate NavigatedEventHandler(object sender, NavigationEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationService | Navigating | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigatingCancelEventArgs>? Navigating` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigatingCancelEventHandler Navigating` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate NavigatingCancelEventHandler(object sender, NavigatingCancelEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationService | NavigationFailed | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationFailedEventArgs>? NavigationFailed` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationFailedEventHandler NavigationFailed` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate NavigationFailedEventHandler(object sender, NavigationFailedEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationService | NavigationProgress | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationProgressEventArgs>? NavigationProgress` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationProgressEventHandler NavigationProgress` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate NavigationProgressEventHandler(object sender, NavigationProgressEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationService | NavigationStopped | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? NavigationStopped` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationStoppedEventHandler NavigationStopped` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate NavigationStoppedEventHandler(object sender, NavigationEventArgs e) and use it as the event type. |
| NavigationWindow | BackStack | `public System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<JournalEntry> BackStack { get; }` | `public System.Collections.IEnumerable BackStack { get; }` | return-type | Low severity: Jalium returns the more specific IEnumerable<JournalEntry> (assignable to IEnumerable). | NavigationWindow | ForwardStack | `public System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<JournalEntry> ForwardStack { get; }` | `public System.Collections.IEnumerable ForwardStack { get; }` | return-type | Low severity: same as BackStack; widen to System.Collections.IEnumerable for exact parity. |
| NavigationWindow | FragmentNavigation | `public event System.EventHandler<FragmentNavigationEventArgs>? FragmentNavigation` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.FragmentNavigationEventHandler FragmentNavigation` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated FragmentNavigationEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | LoadCompleted | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? LoadCompleted` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.LoadCompletedEventHandler LoadCompleted` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated LoadCompletedEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | Navigated | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? Navigated` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigatedEventHandler Navigated` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated NavigatedEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | Navigating | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigatingCancelEventArgs>? Navigating` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigatingCancelEventHandler Navigating` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated NavigatingCancelEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | NavigationFailed | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationFailedEventArgs>? NavigationFailed` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationFailedEventHandler NavigationFailed` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated NavigationFailedEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | NavigationProgress | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationProgressEventArgs>? NavigationProgress` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationProgressEventHandler NavigationProgress` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated NavigationProgressEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| NavigationWindow | NavigationStopped | `public event System.EventHandler<NavigationEventArgs>? NavigationStopped` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.NavigationStoppedEventHandler NavigationStopped` | kind-mismatch | Use the dedicated NavigationStoppedEventHandler delegate rather than EventHandler<T>. |
| PageFunction<T> | PageFunction<T> | `public abstract class PageFunction<T> : Page` | `public partial class PageFunction<T> : System.Windows.Navigation.PageFunctionBase` | kind-mismatch | WPF's PageFunction<T> is a concrete, instantiable class (public parameterless ctor); Jalium marks it abstract and derives from Page instead of the (missing) PageFunctionBase. Introduce PageFunctionBase, derive from it, and make PageFunction<T> concrete with a public ctor. |
| PageFunction<T> | Return | `public event System.EventHandler<ReturnEventArgs<T>>? Return` | `public event System.Windows.Navigation.ReturnEventHandler<T> Return` | kind-mismatch | Define delegate ReturnEventHandler<T>(object sender, ReturnEventArgs<T> e) and use it as the event type instead of EventHandler<T>. |
| PageFunction<T> | OnReturn | `protected void OnReturn(ReturnEventArgs<T> e)` | `protected virtual void OnReturn(System.Windows.Navigation.ReturnEventArgs<T> e)` | kind-mismatch | WPF's OnReturn is virtual; Jalium's is non-virtual. Add the virtual modifier. |
| ReturnEventArgs<T> | Result | `public T? Result { get; }` | `public T Result { get; set; }` | accessibility | WPF's Result is settable (public get/set); Jalium exposes it get-only. Add a public setter to match. |
| JumpItemRejectionReason | JumpItemRejectionReason.NoRegisteredHandler | `NoRegisteredHandler = 3 (implicit ordinal; declared last)` | `NoRegisteredHandler = 2` | default-value | Reorder members or assign explicit values None=0, InvalidItem=1, NoRegisteredHandler=2, RemovedByUser=3 to match WPF ordinals. |
| JumpItemRejectionReason | JumpItemRejectionReason.RemovedByUser | `RemovedByUser = 2 (implicit ordinal; declared 3rd)` | `RemovedByUser = 3` | default-value | Reorder members or assign explicit values so RemovedByUser = 3 as in WPF. |
| JumpList | JumpList | `public sealed class JumpList  (implements no interface)` | `public sealed class JumpList : System.ComponentModel.ISupportInitialize` | kind-mismatch | Declare `: System.ComponentModel.ISupportInitialize`. BeginInit()/EndInit() already exist, so the type just needs to advertise the interface so XAML/ISupportInitialize consumers can use it. |
| JumpList | JumpItems | `public IList<JumpItem> JumpItems { get; }` | `public System.Collections.Generic.List<System.Windows.Shell.JumpItem> JumpItems { get; }` | return-type | Change property type from IList<JumpItem> to the concrete List<JumpItem> to match WPF (WPF exposes List<T>-specific surface e.g. AddRange). |
| JumpList | GetJumpList | `public static JumpList? GetJumpList(object application)` | `public static System.Windows.Shell.JumpList GetJumpList(System.Windows.Application application)` | param-type | Change parameter type from object to Jalium.UI.Controls.Application (that type exists) so the API is type-safe like WPF. |
| JumpList | SetJumpList | `public static void SetJumpList(object application, JumpList value)` | `public static void SetJumpList(System.Windows.Application application, System.Windows.Shell.JumpList value)` | param-type | Change first parameter type from object to Jalium.UI.Controls.Application. |
| ResizeGripDirection | ResizeGripDirection.Right | `Right = 5 (implicit ordinal)` | `Right = 4` | default-value | Assign explicit WPF values: None=0, TopLeft=1, Top=2, TopRight=3, Right=4, BottomRight=5, Bottom=6, BottomLeft=7, Left=8. |
| ResizeGripDirection | ResizeGripDirection.BottomRight | `BottomRight = 8 (implicit ordinal; declared last non-Caption)` | `BottomRight = 5` | default-value | Assign explicit WPF values so BottomRight = 5. |
| ResizeGripDirection | ResizeGripDirection.Bottom | `Bottom = 7 (implicit ordinal)` | `Bottom = 6` | default-value | Assign explicit WPF values so Bottom = 6. |
| ResizeGripDirection | ResizeGripDirection.BottomLeft | `BottomLeft = 6 (implicit ordinal)` | `BottomLeft = 7` | default-value | Assign explicit WPF values so BottomLeft = 7. |
| ResizeGripDirection | ResizeGripDirection.Left | `Left = 4 (implicit ordinal)` | `Left = 8` | default-value | Assign explicit WPF values so Left = 8. |
| TaskbarItemInfo | TaskbarItemInfo | `public sealed class TaskbarItemInfo : DependencyObject` | `public sealed class TaskbarItemInfo : System.Windows.Freezable` | kind-mismatch | Derive from Jalium.UI.Freezable (exists at Jalium.UI.Core/Freezable.cs, which declares `protected abstract Freezable CreateInstanceCore()`) instead of DependencyObject, and add the CreateInstanceCore override. As a plain DependencyObject the type loses Freeze()/clone/cross-thread-share semantics WPF guarantees. |
| TaskbarItemProgressState | TaskbarItemProgressState.Indeterminate | `Indeterminate = 2 (implicit ordinal)` | `Indeterminate = 1` | default-value | Swap declaration order (Indeterminate before Normal) or assign explicit values None=0, Indeterminate=1, Normal=2, Error=3, Paused=4 to match WPF. |
| TaskbarItemProgressState | TaskbarItemProgressState.Normal | `Normal = 1 (implicit ordinal)` | `Normal = 2` | default-value | Swap declaration order or assign explicit values so Normal = 2 as in WPF. |
| ThumbButtonInfo | ThumbButtonInfo | `public sealed class ThumbButtonInfo : DependencyObject` | `public sealed class ThumbButtonInfo : System.Windows.Freezable, System.Windows.Input.ICommandSource` | kind-mismatch | Derive from Jalium.UI.Freezable and implement ICommandSource. NOTE: Jalium has NO ICommandSource interface (absent tree-wide) - it must be added first; the interface pulls in the missing CommandTarget/CommandTargetProperty members. Add CreateInstanceCore override once on Freezable. |
| ThumbButtonInfoCollection | ThumbButtonInfoCollection | `public sealed class ThumbButtonInfoCollection : System.Collections.ObjectModel.ObservableCollection<ThumbButtonInfo>` | `public class ThumbButtonInfoCollection : System.Windows.FreezableCollection<System.Windows.Shell.ThumbButtonInfo>` | kind-mismatch | Derive from Jalium.UI.FreezableCollection<ThumbButtonInfo> (exists at Jalium.UI.Core/Freezable.cs; constraint T:DependencyObject is satisfied) instead of ObservableCollection<T>, and add CreateInstanceCore override. Also un-seal to match WPF. Current ObservableCollection base loses Freezable/DP semantics and Freezable-collection change propagation. |
| WindowChrome | WindowChrome | `public sealed class WindowChrome : DependencyObject` | `public class WindowChrome : System.Windows.Freezable` | kind-mismatch | Derive from Jalium.UI.Freezable and add the CreateInstanceCore override; also un-seal to match WPF (WPF WindowChrome is not sealed). As a plain DependencyObject it loses Freeze()/clone semantics. |
